expectations.text: Explains CA-based architecture (Infisical generates CA key pair, signs certificates)
expectations.evidence: "Infisical generates a CA key pair when you create the dynamic secret...Your servers trust the CA by installing its public key in /etc/ssh/sshd_config"

expectations.text: Shows correct config fields (allowed principals, key algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Configuration table shows 'Allowed Principals' (e.g., ubuntu,deploy,root) and 'Key Algorithm' (ED25519 recommended)

expectations.text: Recommends ED25519 as the key algorithm
expectations.evidence: "Key Algorithm: ED25519 (Recommended for modern servers; alternatives: RSA 2048/4096, ECDSA P-256/P-384)"

expectations.text: Shows target host setup with TrustedUserCAKeys in sshd_config
Correctly states certificates CANNOT be renewed (must create new lease)
expectations.evidence: "Certificates Cannot Be Renewed: The TTL is baked in at signing time. Once a certificate expires, it's no longer valid. Your team must request a new lease for a fresh certificate"

expectations.text: Shows correct SSH usage command with -i key.pem and -o CertificateFile=cert.pub
